created: YYYY-MM-DD
modified: YYYY-MM-DD
last_reviewed: YYYY-MM-DD

# <Product Name> Product Brief / PRD

## Elevator Pitch

`Two to three sentences summarizing what this product is, who it serves, and the core promise.`

## Problem Or Opportunity

`What problem, need, or opportunity this product exists to address.`

## Primary Users

`Who this product is for. Keep lightweight at first; add distinct user archetypes only when they materially change product direction or tradeoffs.`

## Core Outcomes

`What users should be able to achieve because this product exists.`

## Current Scope

`What product territory is currently in scope. Keep this product-facing, not implementation-facing.`

## Non-Goals

`What is intentionally out of scope, deferred, or not part of the product identity. Use this to prevent scope creep; do not turn it into a roadmap.`

## Product Principles

`Durable product-level tradeoff guidance for future humans and agents.`

## Core Functionality

`List what the app does at a product-capability level. Prefer one-line bullets; expand only when needed. If a capability needs deeper product explanation across multiple Epics, create features/<feature-slug>.md and optionally link it here. Do not turn this into SDD changes, Epic details, Stories, Requirements, Scenarios, a roadmap, or an implementation checklist.`

## Market / Competition / Monetization

`Optional for personal/internal tools. For public or potentially monetizable products, summarize market, competitors/substitutes, differentiation, buyer/user, pricing assumptions, and unresolved monetization questions. Split into separate sections only when needed.`

## Related Product Briefs

`Optional. Link only to related PRDs when suite, platform, companion-product, or sub-product context matters. Do not use this as a project navigation index.`

## Open Questions

`Product-level questions that still need a decision. Technical questions belong in SDD design.md, ADRs, Epic docs, tasks.md, or app docs unless they directly affect product identity.`
